document.masterDocTitle: Afrotropical Asilidae (Diptera) 32. A Revision Of Anypodetus Hermann, 1907 With The Description Of Three New Species (Laphriinae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Afrotropical Asilidae (Diptera) 32. A Revision Of Anypodetus Hermann, 1907 With The Description Of Three New Species (Laphriinae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Natal Museum, P. Bag 9070, Pietermaritzburg, South Africa

subSubSection.paragraph:
Anypodetus semirufus Engel, 1924: 104
;
Anypodetus maculipennis Ricardo, 1925: 242
.

Notes : Type material not seen:
A. fascipennis
- 1♂, South Africa, Willowmore, 1.1.1907, Dr Brauns & 2♂1♀, Delarey, Dr Brauns, types in ZSMC;
A. semirufus
- 3♂1♀, South Africa, Willowmore, 1.I. - 5.I.1922, Dr Brauns. Engel (1924) did not indicate the type status of each specimen. If these are syntypes it will be necessary to designate lectotypes for both
fascipennis
and
semirufus
. I have seen many specimens from both Willowmore and Deleray collected by Brauns.
A. maculipennis
- 1♂1♀ syntypes (
maculipennis
), Zimbabwe, Saw Mills (and other material from same locality) apparently in the National Museum of Zimbabwe, Bulawayo. I have seen other material from this locality.

caption.paragraph:
Figs 45-47.
Anypodetus species.
45.
A. fascipennis Engel
, tip of left hind tarsus. 46.
A. unicolor Oldroyd
, egg dissected from female abdomen. 47.
A. leucothrix
sp. n. , scutellum, dorsal view. Scale lines = 1 mm.

subSubSection.paragraph:
A. fascipennis
: Examination of the full series of specimens at my disposal supports acceptance of the synonymy of
semirufus Engel
and
maculipennis Ricardo
as suggested by Oldroyd (1974) . Although I have not seen Ricardo’s syntypes of
maculipennis
, I here designate the male as lectotype and the female as paralectotype .
